agency: Fire Department of the City of New York (FDNY)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/fdny (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace) and the FDNY Business permitting portal at fires.fdnycloud.org, identified as an Accela Civic Platform (Citizen Access) application (`/CitizenAccess/Default.aspx`, Accela CSP allow-list, Datadog RUM) fronted by Cloudflare + an Azure Application Gateway. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Fire Department of New York City (FDNY)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 17 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) informational site + FDNY Business on a rented Accela Civic Platform (fires.fdnycloud.org, Cloudflare + Azure App Gateway, Datadog RUM)
meta.netnew: FirePermitApplication — apply for and track a fire permit / Certificate of Operation / inspection request via FDNY Business
meta.tagline: FDNY publishes its firehouses, incidents, inspections, and violations generously on Open Data, but every business transaction is trapped in a rented Accela permitting cloud with no API.
meta.headline: Put an owned, agent-native API in front of the rented FDNY Business (Accela) portal so applying for a fire permit is a machine-readable call, not a vendor-SaaS screen.

scorecard_notes.platform: informational site on shared NYC.gov Livesite; the transaction platform is a rented Accela Civic Platform SaaS
scorecard_notes.siteApi: nyc.gov/site/fdny is content-only — no content API
scorecard_notes.openData: 17 datasets, generous — firehouses, minute-level fire & EMS dispatch, inspections, violations, certificates
scorecard_notes.coreData: reference & incident data strong, but live permit / C of F / inspection status is only historical snapshots; current status is behind Accela
scorecard_notes.writeApi: no write API; permits, C of F, and inspection requests are Accela UI / paper only
scorecard_notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface exists today
scorecard_notes.ownership: incident-data pipeline is owned; the business transaction system of record is a rented commercial SaaS (Accela)

headline_findings: FDNY is a split domain: an informational NYC.gov site (Akamai/nginx/Livesite/Dynatrace) plus FDNY Business (fires.fdnycloud.org), a rented Accela Civic Platform (Citizen Access) permitting application fronted by Cloudflare and an Azure Application Gateway — with no public API.

The reference and operational data is unusually OPEN: 17 NYC Open Data datasets covering firehouses, minute-level Fire and EMS incident dispatch, Bureau of Fire Prevention inspections, active violation orders, certificates of fitness, building vacate lists, and fire-cause investigations.

But the business SERVICE layer has no machine-readable surface: applying for a fire permit, holding a Certificate of Fitness/Operation, scheduling an inspection, or answering a violation happens only inside the rented Accela portal or on paper.
The Open Data cut is historical/snapshot: inspections, violations, certificates, and building summaries are all published as '(Historical)' extracts — the live regulatory state lives inside Accela.
Net-new write surface: submit a fire permit application (FirePermitApplication) — the core FDNY Business transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 17 FDNY datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open FDNY API — reference/operational data only.

FDNY Business — fire permits, Certificates of Fitness/Operation, inspection scheduling, violation response. Accela Citizen Access (.aspx), Datadog RUM; no JSON/OpenAPI. Fronted by Cloudflare + Azure Application Gateway.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
x-akamai-transformed / server-timing on the informational site.
Accela portal ships a Datadog browser-agent CSP report-uri.

fruit.name: Firehouse Listing
fruit.notes: Core physical unit. Facility name/address, BIN/BBL, and full geography spine (community board, council, census tract, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: Fire & EMS Incident Dispatch
opendata_match: Incidents Responded to by Fire Companies tm6d-hbzd (24c)
Bureau of Fire Investigations - Fire Causes ii3r-svjz
fruit.notes: Two parallel minute-level dispatch feeds (Fire + EMS) with response-timing measures, alarm-box/alarm-level (fire) and call-type/severity/hospital-transport (EMS). The most-viewed operational data.

fruit.name: Bureau of Fire Prevention Inspections
opendata_match: Mandatory Inspections by Fire Companies kfgh-h6re (11c)
fruit.notes: Three inspection programs (BFP, risk-based, mandatory fire-company) keyed on premises account (ACCT_NUM) + BIN/BBL. Published only as historical snapshots; scheduling a NEW inspection has no API.

fruit.name: Active Violation Orders
opendata_match: NYC Fire Department Building Vacate List n5xc-7jfa (11c)
fruit.notes: Violation id/number, cited law, action, premises account + BIN/BBL. Answering or curing a violation is an Accela portal transaction with no API.

fruit.name: Certificates of Fitness
fruit.notes: C of F number/type, holder, expiry, premises. Read-only snapshot; applying for or renewing a C of F happens in FDNY Business.

fruit.name: Apply for a fire permit
fruit.notes: The core FDNY Business transaction — apply for a general fire permit, Place of Assembly Certificate of Operation, torch/hot-work, fireworks, or storage permit. Lives only in the rented Accela Citizen Access portal or on paper. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Hold / renew a Certificate of Fitness
fruit.notes: Apply for / renew a C of F (exam, credential) — Accela-only; no API. Folded into the FirePermitApplication write surface (permitType = Certificate of Fitness).

fruit.name: Schedule an inspection
fruit.notes: Request/schedule an FDNY inspection — Accela-only; no API. Represented as FirePermitApplication permitType = Inspection Request.

fruit.name: Answer a violation
fruit.notes: Respond to / cure a violation and pay penalties — Accela-only; no API. An alternative net-new write workflow adjacent to permit application.
